</traits>

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Woodland Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 40

You're adapted to life in the forest or the deep jungle, and you know how to climb trees and use foliage to your advantage. When [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) trees, vines, and other foliage, you move at half your Speed on a success and at full Speed on a critical success (and you move at full Speed on a success if you have [Quick Climb](/Feats.aspx?ID=821)). This doesn't affect you if you're using a climb Speed.

 You can always use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) action when you are within forest terrain to gain cover, even if you're not next to an obstacle you can Take Cover behind.

<title level="2">Feats that require Woodland Elf...</title>
[Wandering Heart](/Feats.aspx?ID=986)

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Fruit Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=100)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 42

Your body continually produces small fruits imbued with primal magic. At dawn each day, a new fruit ripens. You or an ally can remove this fruit as an Interact action. If a living creature that can derive sustenance from fruit consumes it as an Interact action within the next hour, they regain 1d8 Hit Points, plus an additional 1d8 Hit Points for every 2 of your levels beyond 1st. This effect has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), and [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134) traits.
name: Fruit Leshy

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Lotus Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=101)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 42

You effortlessly float on the surface of water. You can walk on the surface of still water and other non-damaging liquids, moving at half your normal Speed. You can attempt to walk along the surface of flowing water as well, still moving at half Speed, but doing so requires a successful [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) using the DC of a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) check to move through the water; on a failure, you fall into the water. This Acrobatics check doesn't use an action.
name: Lotus Leshy

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Root Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=102)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 42

Your body is made from hardy roots that attach you firmly to the ground. You gain 10 Hit Points from your ancestry instead of 8. You can go without sunlight for 2 weeks before you begin to [starve](/Rules.aspx?ID=537). You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to move you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Root Leshy

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 45

You move easily through thick forest and jungle, using every branch or bush. You can always use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) action when in forest or jungle terrain to gain cover, even if you're not next to an obstacle you would normally be able to Take Cover behind. You gain the [Terrain Stalker](/Feats.aspx?ID=852) feat, even if you're not trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15), and you must choose underbrush as your chosen terrain.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Chameleon Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 43

The color of your hair and skin is mutable, possibly due to latent magic. You can slowly change the vibrancy and the exact color, and the coloration can be different across your body, allowing you to create patterns or other colorful designs. It takes a single action for minor localized shifts and up to an hour for dramatic shifts throughout your body. While you're asleep, the colors shift on their own in tune with your dreams, giving you an unusual coloration each morning. When you're in an area where your coloration is roughly similar to the environment (for instance, forest green in a forest), you can use the single action to make minor localized shifts designed to help you blend into your surroundings. This grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks until your surroundings shift in coloration or pattern.

<title level="2">Feats that require Chameleon Gnome...</title>
[Vibrant Display](/Feats.aspx?ID=994)
name: Chameleon Gnome

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 54

Your incredibly small bones allow you to easily compress your body and squeeze through gaps. You gain the [Quick Squeeze](/Feats.aspx?ID=828) feat as a bonus feat, even if you aren't trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1). Tight spaces not tight enough to require the [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) action aren't difficult terrain for you.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Impersonator Android](/Heritages.aspx?ID=114)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 71

Your body was augmented with processes and an appearance intended to blend in with humans more easily. You become trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) (or another skill if you're already trained in Deception). You don't require a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19) when attempting Deception checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6). In addition, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to Impersonate checks to pretend that you're a human version of yourself, rather than an android. This bonus doesn't apply to other checks to Impersonate humans.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 71

You were preprogrammed with a multitude of linguistic proficiencies, likely to act as a translator. You learn two new languages, chosen from common languages and any uncommon languages you have access to. These languages take the same form (signed or spoken) as your other languages. If you select the [Multilingual](/Feats.aspx?ID=814) feat, you learn three new languages instead of two.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Beastkin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=119)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 79

The blood of a beast flows through your veins, granting you the ferocity and might of animals. Only creatures with the [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) trait can take the beastkin versatile heritage. Choose a type of animal such as bat, eagle, shark, spider, tyrannosaurus, wasp, or wolf. This is the type of animal tied to your heritage and is known as your inherent animal. You gain the [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20) and [beastkin](/Traits.aspx?ID=337) traits, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You gain the Change Shape ability. A beastkin's hybrid form is their natural shape. You can choose from beastkin feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Fey-Touched Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 43

The blood of the fey is so strong within you that you're truly one of them. You gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) trait, in addition to the [gnome](/Traits.aspx?ID=80) and [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) traits. Choose one cantrip from the [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) spell list. You can cast this spell as a primal innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up. You can change this cantrip to a different one from the same list once per day by meditating to realign yourself with the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10); this is a 10-minute activity that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
name: Fey-Touched Gnome

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Bright Fetchling](/Heritages.aspx?ID=120)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 85

Your body proves that shadow can't exist without light. Perhaps your eyes glow brighter or your veins occasionally pulse with light. You emit dim light within 5 feet of you. You can activate or suppress this ability as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. Additionally, you can force fragments of your shadow to glow and cast [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58) and [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) as occult innate cantrips. Cantrips are heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.
name: Bright Fetchling

Your predecessors dwelled in the [Shadow Plane's](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) deepest, most treacherous regions. You might cast a denser shadow, have a slower pulse, or find comfort in the embrace of darkness. You gain cold or negative resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), chosen when you gain this heritage.

You come from a line of fetchlings with a closer connection to the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) and [Shadow Planes](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), and you share those realms' affinity with the in-between and unseen. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to locate [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures within 60 feet when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action. Your flat check to target [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) creatures is DC 3 instead of DC 5, and your flat check to target undetected creatures is DC 9 instead of DC 11.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 85

Your ancestors' exposure to the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) wrought great physical changes. Your hair or skin sometimes appears insubstantial, and you're slighter and more agile than other fetchlings. You're Small instead of Medium. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1), the [Quick Squeeze](/Feats.aspx?ID=828) skill feat, and a +1 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics checks to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29). If you would automatically become trained in Acrobatics (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.
name: Wisp Fetchling

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 44

You see all colors as brighter, hear all sounds as richer, and especially smell all scents with incredible detail. You gain a special sense: [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine the exact location of a creature. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from the creature or halve the range if you're upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks whenever you're trying to locate an [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creature that is within the range of your scent.
name: Sensate Gnome

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ifrit](/Heritages.aspx?ID=130)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 101

You descend from [fire elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=43) or bear the mark of the Inner Spheres, and your features illustrate the influence that elemental fire has over you. You gain the [ifrit](/Traits.aspx?ID=301) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You gain resistance to fire equal to half your level (minimum 1), and you treat [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less severe (incredible heat becomes extreme, extreme heat becomes severe, and so on). You can choose from ifrit feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

You can choose from oread feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

</traits>

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The temporary Hit Points from Invigorating Fear last for 1 minute._

You can exert your unsettling presence to subtly [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) others. When you do, Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language. You also gain the Invigorating Fear reaction. Your alternate form is a fox, which has the statistics of 1st-level [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217).

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Wellspring Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 44

Some other source of magic has a greater hold on you than the primal magic of your fey lineage does. This connection might come from an occult plane or an ancient occult song; a deity, celestial, or fiend; magical effluent left behind by a mage war; or ancient rune magic.

 Choose [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), or [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3). You gain one cantrip from that magical tradition's spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate spell at will, as a spell of your chosen tradition. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up. Whenever you gain a primal innate spell from a gnome ancestry feat, change its tradition from primal to your chosen tradition.
name: Wellspring Gnome

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Songbird Strix](/Heritages.aspx?ID=150)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 135

You descend from a talented line of strix storytellers. From the highest mountains and purest landscapes where your lungs fill with glorious clean air, you trill, whistle, and croon sweet songs. While natural sounds make you predisposed to sing, voices make you predisposed to mimicry. You receive a +1 circumstance bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a mimicked voice, where the sound of the voice is the only factor; if you are a master in Deception, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus instead. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to sing; if you are a master in Performance, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus instead.
name: Songbird Strix

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ancient Scale Azarketi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=151)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3, [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393

Your lineage stems from azarketis who remain dedicated to their deep-sea roots. Divorced from land society, you're a foreigner to any world above a thousand fathoms deep. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). Your body is dotted with phosphorescent spots that emit a guiding light and help you communicate. The spots—located primarily on your face, arms, and hands—illuminate a 10-foot radius around you with dim light. You can activate, deactivate, or change the arrangement of lights as an action, which has the [concentration](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=86) trait.
name: Ancient Scale Azarketi

Your heritage traces to azarketis living deep beneath the sea, and you can handle the chilling depths more easily than most. You gain resistance to cold equal to half your level, and you don't treat [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as one degree more severe when you are wet. You adapt to pressure changes from being deep underwater automatically without ill effect.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Mistbreath Azarketi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=153)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3, [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393

You descend from azarketis who migrated to land environments that could support their need for water. Over time, your people adapted to life on land culturally and physically, even resulting in azarketis born with human hair like their Azlanti ancestors. You no longer need to be immersed in water every 24 hours to maintain your skin and can instead mist or wipe your skin with water to live comfortably. Your land Speed is 25 feet, but your swim Speed is only 15 feet.
name: Mistbreath Azarketi

The varied geography of rivers required you to develop advanced physical skills to swim against rapids, leap through cascading waters, and deftly navigate shallow and narrow channels. When you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), you get a critical success instead.

Your ancestors have always had a connection to fire and a thicker skin, which allows you to resist burning. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). You can also recover from being on fire more easily. Your flat check to remove [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is DC 10 instead of DC 15, which is reduced to DC 5 if another creature uses a particularly appropriate action to help.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 107

Your woven exoskeleton rivals the hardest armors that can be found. Your exoskeleton is medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grants a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a speed penalty of –5 feet, and a Strength value of 16, and has the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=203) trait. You can never wear other armor or remove your exoskeleton. You can etch armor runes onto your exoskeleton as normal.

You're a sharp-featured, big-eared gnoll about three feet tall. Many are skeptical that you are in fact a gnoll. Your size is Small instead of Medium. You are trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) (or another skill if you were already trained in Deception). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) when specifically claiming innocence, to Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) checks to uncover such lies, and to initiative checks when you roll Deception for initiative.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Irongut Goblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 47

You can subsist on food that most folks would consider spoiled. You can keep yourself fed with poor meals in a settlement as long as garbage is readily available, without using the [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27) downtime activity. You can eat and drink things when you are [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

 You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against afflictions, against gaining the sickened condition, and to remove the sickened condition. When you roll a success on a Fortitude save affected by this bonus, you get a critical success instead. All these benefits apply only when the affliction or condition resulted from something you ingested.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 119

Your tongue is especially long, and you can launch it with extraordinary range and precision. You can use your tongue to deliver touch-range spells and perform extremely simple Interact actions, such as opening some types of unlocked doors. Your tongue can't perform actions that require fingers or significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish, and you can't use it to hold items.

<title level="2">Feats that require Snaptongue Grippli...</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 119

Your hands and feet exude a film that helps them adhere to surfaces. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude and Reflex DC against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. When ascending trees, vines, and other foliage, if you roll a success on the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), you get a critical success instead.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Stronggut Shisk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=182)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 123

Your metabolism is slow, allowing you to go for days without food and process maladies at a slower rate. You can go for 1 week without food before you begin to [starve](/Rules.aspx?ID=537). Additionally, the onset times and lengths of stages for all [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) that affect you are increased by 50%. If the onset time or stage is instant or lasts only 1 round, this ability has no effect.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 36

You were designed to serve as a scout or assassin and have a body resembling a pack hunter like a large cat or wolf. Though you typically move like a quadruped, you can still stand and fight like a biped, allowing you to use all equipment normally. Your quadruped design allows you to move quickly; if you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Toy Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=190)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 63

You have the form of a child's tiny toy or doll, but you don't let your small size impede your joy of life. Instead of Small, your size is Tiny. Like other Tiny creatures, you don't automatically receive lesser cover from being in a larger creature's space, but circumstances might allow you to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90). You can purchase weapons, armor, and other items for your size with the same statistics as normal gear, except that melee weapons have a reach of 0 for you (or a reach 5 feet shorter than normal if they have the reach trait). You can enter another creature's space, which is important because you must usually enter a creature's space to attack it with melee Strikes! Remember to [adjust the Bulk of items and your Bulk limit for Tiny size](/Rules.aspx?ID=256). The information on how to handle Tiny PCs, including rules for attempting to ride on other characters, appears [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=1458).
name: Toy Poppet

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Windup Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=191)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 63

You're made primarily of soft metals, such as tin or silver, and your life force dwells within an exceptional array of clockworks deep in your body. Provided you wind metal tabs on your body a few times each day, you don't need food or water to survive. You must still breathe to ventilate your internal mechanisms and sleep to give your mechanisms rest, just like other poppets.
name: Windup Poppet

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 63

You were wished to life, either by a powerful spellcaster or by the earnest desire of an innocent person who loved you very much. You're living proof that a hopeful spirit can overcome any obstacle. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) or [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect, you get a critical success instead.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ancient Scale Azarketi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=193)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393, [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3

Your lineage stems from azarketis who remain dedicated to their deep-sea roots. Divorced from land society, you're a foreigner to any world above a thousand fathoms deep. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). Your body is dotted with phosphorescent spots that emit a guiding light and help you communicate. The spots—located primarily on your face, arms, and hands—illuminate a 10-foot radius around you with dim light. You can activate, deactivate, or change the arrangement of lights as an action, which has the [concentration](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
name: Ancient Scale Azarketi
primary_source_raw: Absalom, City of Lost Omens pg. 393

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393, [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3

Your heritage traces to azarketis living deep beneath the sea, and you can handle the chilling depths more easily than most. You gain resistance to cold equal to half your level, and you don't treat [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as one degree more severe when you are wet. You adapt to pressure changes from being deep underwater automatically without ill effect.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Mistbreath Azarketi](/Heritages.aspx?ID=195)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393, [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3

You descend from azarketis who migrated to land environments that could support their need for water. Over time, your people adapted to life on land culturally and physically, even resulting in azarketis born with human hair like their Azlanti ancestors. You no longer need to be immersed in water every 24 hours to maintain your skin and can instead mist or wipe your skin with water to live comfortably. Your land Speed is 25 feet, but your swim Speed is only 15 feet.
name: Mistbreath Azarketi
primary_source_raw: Absalom, City of Lost Omens pg. 393

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 393, [Azarketi Ancestry Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=76) pg. 3

You come from azarketis who abandoned the oceans for the fresh water of inland life. The varied geography of rivers required you to develop advanced physical skills to swim against rapids, leap through cascading waters, and deftly navigate shallow and narrow channels. When you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), you get a critical success instead.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Fodder Skeleton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=199)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 55

[Humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), and those who are physically closest to them, are the favored material of necromancers. They are readily available, and their physiology has been studied the most extensively, so these skeletons can be easily mass-produced. Despite their name, fodder skeletons make rather mobile foot soldiers due to how efficiently they transfer necromantic energy within their bodies. You have a base Speed of 30 feet, instead of 25 feet.
name: Fodder Skeleton

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Compact Skeleton](/Heritages.aspx?ID=200)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 55

For tasks that require a lighter touch, [gnome](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3), [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4), and [halfling](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=5) remains are more suitable than a larger creature's. These smaller, nimbler skeletons fit well in a traveling chest, can be forced through tight spaces, and more easily avoid detection. Your size is Small instead of Medium. You gain the [Quick Squeeze](/Feats.aspx?ID=828) feat, even if you aren't trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1). Tight spaces not tight enough to require the [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) activity aren't difficult terrain for you.
name: Compact Skeleton

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 56

Inhuman skeletons are often created for both their deadly physical attacks and their terrifying appearances. Beasts and monstrous humanoids with the strangest skeletons tend to be the centerpiece of the collection of a necromancer skilled enough to raise one. You gain a claw, horn, tail, or wing unarmed attack that deals 1d6 damage. A claw deals slashing damage, a horn deals piercing damage, and a tail or wing deals bludgeoning damage. This unarmed attack is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and has the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Reflection](/Heritages.aspx?ID=203)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

**Source** [Dark Archive](/Sources.aspx?ID=129) pg. 119

You were created as a duplicate of another creature, intentionally or accidentally, though you might not know of your origins. Other than a minor mark or two, you look just like your progenitor. You gain the [reflection](/Traits.aspx?ID=431) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You don't need to attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) your progenitor unless you're interacting with people who know them personally or you do something known to be out of character for them. The GM might require you to roll a Deception check in other circumstances, such as if you're mirror-risen and interacting with someone who has seen an accurate likeness of your progenitor and might notice a distinguishing feature on the reverse side. You can choose from reflection feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Reflection

Other than a minor mark or two, you look just like your progenitor. You gain the reflection trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You don't need to attempt Deception checks to Impersonate your progenitor unless you're interacting with people who know them personally or you do something known to be out of character for them. The GM might require you to roll a Deception check in other circumstances, such as if you're mirror-risen and interacting with someone who has seen an accurate likeness of your progenitor and might notice a distinguishing feature on the reverse side. You can choose from reflection feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Cataphract Fleshwarp](/Heritages.aspx?ID=204)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 28

Your battle-hardened skeleton is laced with additional cartilage and muscle to help bear armor across vast areas of operation, and your skin is bedecked with tiny spurs of pliable, resilient keratin to optimize the grip and weight distribution of your armor. You gain the [Armor Proficiency](/Feats.aspx?ID=755) feat. If your class makes you trained in all types of armor, you instead become trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) (or a skill of your choice if you're already trained in Athletics) and gain the [Armor Assist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2109) skill feat.

<title level="2">Feats that require Cataphract Fleshwarp...</title>
[Embodied Dreadnought Subjectivity](/Feats.aspx?ID=3927), [Embodied Legionary Subjectivity](/Feats.aspx?ID=3926)

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Discarded Fleshwarp](/Heritages.aspx?ID=205)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 28

The biomancers and mutagenists who warped your form labeled you a “discard on discovery”—a euphemism for destroying you on sight. An anomaly among anomalies, your body stubbornly repudiates the efforts of fleshcrafters seeking to mold you to their grandiose visions, and your immune responses blunt the worst effects of unwanted fleshwarping attempts. If you roll a success on a saving throw against a [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) effect, you get a critical success instead.
name: Discarded Fleshwarp

The undulations of your body's cilia are hyper-attuned to wellsprings of magic; they trill and thrum with insights into occult energies, and their vibrations alert you to the presence of secrets. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11). If you're trained in Occultism, you instead become trained in another skill of your choice. You also gain the [Oddity Identification](/Feats.aspx?ID=817) skill feat.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 36

Most ghorans have bodies of pliable vines and tough stems, but you're covered in sturdy, rough bark and your legs are stable as roots. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against effects that would [grab](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) you, [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) you, or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus on [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28).

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 48

You bear the hooded head of a spitting cobra, and like such cobras, you can shoot streams of venom from your mouth. You gain a venomous spit ranged [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack with a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 10 feet that deals 1d4 poison damage. On a critical hit, the target takes [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to the number of weapon damage dice. Your spit doesn't have a weapon group or a critical specialization effect.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 48

You stand out from most nagaji, with the upper body of a beautiful human and the lower body of a green or white snake. Legends claim your ancestors were faithful snakes uplifted by [Nalinivati](/Deities.aspx?ID=181) rather than nagaji created by the goddess. Instead of a fangs unarmed attack, you have a tail attack that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group, and has the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against effects that would [grab](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) you, [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) you, or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

Your scales are medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grant a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a Speed penalty of –5 feet, a Strength value of 16, and have the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=203) trait. You can never wear other armor or remove your scales. You can etch armor runes onto your scales.

Accustomed to a calm life in the hills, your people find rest and relaxation especially replenishing, particularly when indulging in creature comforts. When you regain Hit Points overnight, add your level to the Hit Points regained. When anyone uses the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) skill to [Treat your Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57), you can eat a snack to add your level to the Hit Points you regain from their treatment.

Your vanara ancestors might have been born in [Ragdya's](/Deities.aspx?ID=194) image, but they found survival only in service to his enemy: the red-faced [Lahkgya](/Deities.aspx?ID=191). Keepers of traditions and tellers of ancient tales, your family traces its lineage to those born directly from [Ragdya's](/Deities.aspx?ID=194) whims. When you speak, others are inclined to listen to you, perhaps due to the divine spark of your connection to Ragdya. You gain one cantrip from the [divine spell list](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2); this cantrip can't deal damage or otherwise cause direct harm. You can cast this spell as a divine innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.

Your ancestors were forced into labor by the [rakshasa](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=85) immortal Ravana, the First and the Last. Although their cleverness while in captivity saved countless lives, and they're bound no longer, the time marked your family with scars upon the mind that will never truly heal, even across the generations. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects, and whenever you roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead.

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 47

You descend from wood elementals or have some other heritage influenced by the elemental [Plane of Wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508). You might have green, mossy skin, vines that grow from your head instead of hair, or thin appendages that resemble twigs. You gain the [ardande](/Traits.aspx?ID=498) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You also gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision.

 You can choose from ardande feats, geniekin feats, and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Talos](/Heritages.aspx?ID=233)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 51

Your physical features reflect the influence a [zuhra](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=50) or other metal elemental has had over your bloodline. Your skin likely has a metallic sheen, ranging from the dull luster of raw ore to the gleaming polish of a new blade, with the wear of age or hardship taking the form of tarnish, rust, or scouring. Your hair might resemble spun gold, coiled steel, copper wiring, or braided chains. You gain the [talos](/Traits.aspx?ID=507) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You gain resistance to electricity equal to half your level (minimum 1). You can cast the [_detect metal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1368) cantrip as an arcane innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

 You can choose from talos feats, geniekin feats, and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Talos

<traits>

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Rock Dwarf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=237)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 43

Your ancestors lived and worked among the ancient stones of the mountains or the depths of the earth. This makes you solid as a rock when you plant your feet. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to force you to move or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). In addition, if any effect would force you to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet or more, you are moved only half the distance.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Strong-Blooded Dwarf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=238)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 43

Your blood runs hearty and strong, and you can shake off toxins. You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and each of your successful saving throws against a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) affliction reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a virulent poison. Each critical success against an ongoing poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent poison.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ancient Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=239)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 47

In your long life, you've dabbled in many paths and many styles. A typical ancient elf is at least 100 years old, though you might be younger at the GM's discretion. Choose a class other than your own. You gain the [multiclass dedication](/Traits.aspx?ID=659) feat for that class, even though you don't meet its level prerequisite. You must still meet its other prerequisites to gain the feat.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Seer Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=242)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 47

You have an inborn ability to detect and understand magical phenomena. You can cast the [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) cantrip as an arcane innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) and to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) of a magical nature. These skill actions typically use the [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) skill.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Whisper Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=243)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 47

Your ears are finely tuned, able to detect even the slightest whispers of sound. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures within 30 feet of you. When you target an opponent that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you or hidden from you, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one. This benefit doesn't apply if you can't hear or if the creature is incapable of making sound (for example, if it's affected by a [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) spell).

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Woodland Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=244)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 47

You're adapted to life in the forest, the deep jungle, or a similar environment, and you know how to climb trees and use foliage to your advantage. When [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) trees, vines, and other foliage, you move at half your Speed on a success and at full Speed on a critical success (and you move at full Speed on a success if you have [Quick Climb](/Feats.aspx?ID=821)). This doesn't affect you if you're using a climb Speed.

 You can always use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90) action when you are within forest terrain to gain cover, even if you're not next to an obstacle you can Take Cover behind.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Chameleon Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=245)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 51

The color of your hair and skin is mutable, possibly due to latent magic from First World influences or lingering illusion effects. You can slowly change the vibrancy and the exact color, and the coloration can be different across your body, allowing you to create patterns or other colorful designs. It takes a single action for minor localized shifts and up to an hour for dramatic shifts throughout your body. While you're asleep, the colors shift on their own in tune with your dreams, giving you an unusual coloration each morning. When you're in an area where your coloration is roughly similar to the environment (for instance, forest green in a forest), you can use the single action to make minor localized shifts designed to help you blend into your surroundings. This grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks until your surroundings shift in coloration or pattern.
name: Chameleon Gnome

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Fey-touched Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=246)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 51

The blood of the fey flows through your veins, saturating you with their magic and making you truly one of them. You gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) trait, in addition to the [gnome](/Traits.aspx?ID=80) and [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) traits. Choose one cantrip from the [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) spell list. You can cast this spell as a primal innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. You can change this cantrip to a different one from the same list once per day by meditating to realign yourself with the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10); this is a 10-minute activity that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
name: Fey-touched Gnome

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Sensate Gnome](/Heritages.aspx?ID=247)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 51

You see all colors as brighter, hear all sounds as richer, and especially smell all scents with incredible detail. You gain a special sense: [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine the exact location of a creature. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from the creature or halve the range if you're upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks whenever you're trying to locate an undetected creature that is within the range of your scent.

Some other source of magic has a greater hold on you than the primal magic of your fey lineage does. This connection might come from an occult plane or an ancient occult song; a deity, celestial, or fiend; magical effluent left behind by a mage war; or ancient rune magic.

 Choose [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), or [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3). You gain one cantrip from that magical tradition's spell list. Whenever you gain a primal innate spell from a gnome ancestry feat, change its tradition from primal to your chosen tradition.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 55

Your ancestors have always had a connection to fire and a thicker skin, which allows you to resist burning. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level minimum 1). You can also recover from being on fire more easily. Your flat check to remove [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is DC 10 instead of DC 15, which is reduced to DC 5 if another creature uses a particularly appropriate action to help.
name: Charhide Goblin

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Irongut Goblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=251)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 55

You can subsist on food that most folks would consider spoiled. You can keep yourself fed with poor meals in a settlement as long as garbage is readily available, without using the [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=27) downtime activity. You can eat and drink things when you are [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

 You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against afflictions, against gaining the sickened condition, and to remove the sickened condition. When you roll a success on a Fortitude save affected by this bonus, you get a critical success instead. All these benefits apply only when the affliction or condition resulted from something you ingested.
name: Irongut Goblin

You are acclimated to living in frigid lands and have skin ranging from sky blue to navy in color, as well as blue fur. You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). You treat [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less extreme (incredible cold becomes extreme, extreme cold becomes severe, and so on).

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 59

Accustomed to a calm life in the hills, your people find rest and relaxation especially replenishing, particularly when indulging in creature comforts. When you regain Hit Points overnight, add your level to the Hit Points regained. When anyone uses the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) skill to [Treat your Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57), you can eat a snack to add your level to the Hit Points you regain from their treatment.

Either one of your parents was an [elf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2), or one or both were half-elves. You have pointed ears and other telltale signs of elf heritage. You gain the [elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) trait, the [half-elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=85) trait, and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416). In addition, you can select elf, half-elf, and human feats whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

<traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 67

Your body continually produces small fruits imbued with primal magic. At dawn each day, a new fruit ripens. You or an ally can remove this fruit as an Interact action. If a living creature that can derive sustenance from fruit consumes it as an Interact action within the next hour, they regain 1d8 Hit Points, plus an additional 1d8 Hit Points for every 2 of your levels beyond 1st. This effect has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) and [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) traits.

Drawing the item grants this other action the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Lotus Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=268)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 67

You effortlessly float on the surface of water. You can walk on the surface of still water and other non-damaging liquids, moving at half your normal Speed. You can also attempt to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) to move across the surface of flowing water, using the DC of a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) check to move through the water. When you do this, you can't move faster than half your Speed, and if you fail or critically fail, you fall into the water rather than the normal effects.
name: Lotus Leshy

Your body is made from hardy roots that attach you firmly to the ground. You gain 10 Hit Points from your ancestry instead of 8. You can go without sunlight for 2 weeks before you begin to starve. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to Reposition, [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

In addition, you can select orc, half-orc, and human feats whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

You were exposed to powerful necromantic energies that should have killed you—but you survived. Your skin is cold, clammy, and gray. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects with the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) trait.

<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 76

Your mother was a hag. Your eyes' heterochromia is the most obvious signifier of this parentage, but you likely also have a slighter build, paler skin, and darker hair than most members of your other parent's ancestry. You gain the changeling trait. You also gain low-light vision, or you gain darkvision if your ancestry already has low-light vision. You can select from changeling feats and feats from your other parent's ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Coral Athamaru](/Heritages.aspx?ID=284)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 18

Coral covers patches of your body, which provides a natural layer of defense. Coral athamarus often choose Coral Symbiotes and other related ancestry feats. The coral plates are medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grant a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a Speed penalty of –5 feet, a Strength value of +3, and have the [aquadynamic](/Traits.aspx?ID=792) and [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=556) traits. You can never wear other armor or remove the coral. You can etch armor runes onto the coral.
name: Coral Athamaru

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Hopeful Athamaru](/Heritages.aspx?ID=285)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 18

You have begun to prepare yourself to become an athamaru matriarch, and are likely stepping outside of your community to better prepare yourself as a leader. As part of the physical change, you are substantially taller than you once were. Instead of Medium, your size is Large (see [Large PCs](/Rules.aspx?ID=3270) for rules for Large player characters). In addition, you instill your allies with a feeling of hopefulness. You have a 10-foot aura that grants any ally in it a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598); this is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect.
name: Hopeful Athamaru

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Kaleidoscopic Athamaru](/Heritages.aspx?ID=286)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 18

Your scales are a spectrum of color that shimmers in the light. In areas of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408), you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. You also gain the Dazzle Seeker reaction.

 **Dazzle Seeker** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Trigger** A creature attempts a flat check to target you due to you being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from it; **Effect** You flash your bright scales in the creature's eyes, making it hard for them to pinpoint your exact location. The creature must roll the flat check twice and take the worse result.
name: Kaleidoscopic Athamaru

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 18

Sharp quills on your head can pierce your foes deeply. Most athamarus use such quills for defense, but a well-timed headbash can be devastatingly effective. You gain a quills melee unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. Your quills are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Climbing Animal](/Heritages.aspx?ID=288)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 24

You are an animal whose limbs are adapted to grab, climb, and brachiate. You might be dexterous and ready to use tools like a chimpanzee or otter, or you may simply be a quick climber like a bear, raccoon, or sloth. You have a land Speed of 20 feet, a climb Speed of 20 feet, and one animal attack of your choice (typically claw, fist, or jaws; see the sidebar).

<title level="2">Feats that require Climbing Animal...</title>
[Digger](/Feats.aspx?ID=5317), [Fierce Grasp](/Feats.aspx?ID=5307)

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Flying Animal](/Heritages.aspx?ID=289)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 24

You are an animal that can take flight for long or sustained bursts, such as an eagle, bat, bee, or flying squirrel. The awakening process has disrupted your ability to fly as freely as you once did. What used to be an automatic process is now one that you must apply some thought to until it becomes automatic once again. You can still slow your descent, so you take no [damage from falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402), regardless of the distance you fall. Most flying awakened animals choose the [Take Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=5304) ancestry feat at 1st level to regain a limited ability to fly.

 You have a land Speed of 20 feet and one animal attack of your choice (typically beak, claw, jaws, talon, or wing; see the sidebar).

<title level="2">Feats that require Flying Animal...</title>
[Take Flight](/Feats.aspx?ID=5304)
name: Flying Animal

 You have a land Speed of 30 feet and one animal attack of your choice (typically claw, jaws, or tail; see the sidebar).

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 24 You are an aquatic animal who is most comfortable in the water. You may spend much of your time in the water like an alligator, seal, whale, or dolphin, or you may require the water to breathe like a fish or some crustaceans. You have one animal attack of your choice (typically claw, jaws, or tail; see the sidebar). Choose if you are aquatic or water-dwelling.
  • **Aquatic**: You gain the [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) trait and you have a swim Speed of 30 feet. The aquatic trait means you breathe water but not air, and your bludgeoning and slashing unarmed Strikes don't take the usual –2 penalty for being underwater.
  • **Water-dwelling**: You can [hold your breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) underwater for 10 minutes before needing air. You have a swim Speed of 20 feet, and if you can move on land, you have base Speed of 20 feet.
Feats that require Swimming Animal... [Land Legs](/Feats.aspx?ID=5300)

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Budding Speaker Centaur](/Heritages.aspx?ID=292)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 30

You were born with a spark of magic that could set you on the path to becoming a Greenspeaker or Faithspeaker. Select [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) or [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4). If you selected divine, you're a Faithspeaker. If you selected primal, you're a Greenspeaker. This choice can't be changed. You gain one [cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) from the chosen spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

<title level="2">Feats that require Budding Speaker Centaur...</title>
[Merge with the Source](/Feats.aspx?ID=5341), [Speaker in Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=5330), [Speaker's Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=5335)

You live far, far below the surface of the ocean. Your fish tail might resemble a viperfish or anglerfish, and you might have luminous eyes or translucent skin. Abyssal merfolk have an uncanny reputation, but they can exist in even the most lightless realms. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) and are immune to the crushing pressure of the oceanic depths.

You gain [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense with a range of 30 feet. However, you can smell spilled blood at a range of 120 feet in the air and 500 feet in the water.

You have a remarkable adaptation to hot environments from ancestors who inhabited blazing deserts or volcanic chambers beneath the earth. This grants you fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and you treat [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less extreme (incredible heat becomes extreme, extreme heat becomes severe, and so on).

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Pelagic Merfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=300)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 36

You're a merfolk of the open seas. Your tail is patterned after the silvery tuna and herring that school by the million in your hunting grounds. Water obeys your will, and with a quick word, you can draw a sheathe of it around you. You gain the Shielding Wave action.

 **Shielding Wave** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) You raise a magical barrier of twisting currents. Until the start of your next turn, the first time you take acid or fire damage, you gain resistance to that damage equal to half your level (minimum 1). The resistance is equal to your level instead if you're submerged in water, if you already Cast a Spell with the water trait, or if you used another ability with the water trait this turn.
name: Pelagic Merfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Reef Merfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=301)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 36

You were raised amid colorful corals and schools of tropical fish. Compared to most merfolk, your body is vividly hued with stripes, spots, and patterns like a clownfish or angelfish. You're used to the occasionally toxic denizens of your home and are bothered little by stings or petty poisons. You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and each of your successful saving throws against a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) affliction reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=726) poison. Each critical success against an ongoing poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent poison.
name: Reef Merfolk

The lower half of your body resembles a powerful sailfish, complete with a large dorsal fin. Using this fin, you can move through the water faster and leap farther. Your swim Speed increases to 30 feet. When you attempt a [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check, and you can [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding before attempting the jump.

Your fur is as pale as death, possibly from some connection you or your family has to the afterlife, which lets you supernaturally find your way.  **Raise Slabs**<actions string="Two Actions" /> ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Thick slabs of stone rise around you before tilting over. On a critical failure, the creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). At 3rd level, and every 2 levels thereafter, this damage increases by 1d6.

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 48 Your claws are especially hard and can break through earth and predators alike. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage. Your claws are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719), and [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) traits.
  • **Evolution** Your wrist nodes project magic into a durable digging wedge. You can spend an Interact action to increase your claw unarmed attack's damage to 1d6; grant it the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784), and versatile force traits; and remove the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait. You can spend another action to deactivate the wedge and return your claw to its normal statistics.
  • **Evolution** Your ankle nodes can emit grounding claw spikes of magic to hold you in place. If any effect would [force you to move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet or more, you can choose to move only half the distance. The extra traction from your claw spikes allows you to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) with one hand occupied (or with both hands occupied if you have the [Combat Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=5134) feat). You gain the Trench Digging reaction.
    **Trench Digging** **Trigger** You're subjected to [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451); **Effect** You deploy your claw spikes to churn the earth asunder, creating [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) in each square of ground you're moved through.
name: Breaker Surki

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 49 The top layer of your carapace is especially mobile, and you can unfurl it to catch the air gracefully as you fall. You take no damage from [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402), regardless of the distance you fall.
  • **Evolution** Your shoulder nodes have evolved into a pair of spines that you can energize to project a pair of glowing wings. You can cast [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) as an innate spell once per day, targeting yourself; while your wings are energized, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet.
  • **Evolution** Your shoulder nodes have evolved into a fan of thin membranes that resonate when magic is coursed through them. You gain the Stridulating Song action.
    **Stridulating Song** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You circulate magic through your wings, causing them to vibrate at high speeds such that their scraping produces disruptive sounds. You focus the sound at one creature within 30 feet who must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), or sickened 2 on a critical failure, as the vibrations disrupt its inner equilibrium. You can Sustain the effect for up to 1 minute; the creature can't recover from the sickened condition as long as you continue your song and it can hear you.
name: Elytron Surki

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 49 Your carapace is much denser than that of other surkis, offering you a suit of your very own armor. Your carapace is medium armor in the [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=4) armor group that grants a +4 item bonus to AC, a Dex cap of +1, a check penalty of –2, a Speed penalty of –5 feet, a Strength value of +3, and has the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=556) trait. You can never wear other armor or remove your carapace. You can etch armor runes onto your carapace.
  • **Evolution** The magical circulatory system that runs between your nodes has become a reinforcing network that strengthens your carapace with magical energy. If you're struck by a critical hit that deals physical damage, you can use your reaction to attempt a DC 17 flat check. If successful, the attack becomes a normal hit.
  • **Evolution** Your abdominal nodes can project a field around you that guards you against the type of magic you're most familiar with. You gain the Dampening Harmonics action.
    **Dampening Harmonics** ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You establish a force field that grants you resistance 10 to damage dealt by spells and magical abilities with the trait of your magiphage ability, except for force damage. The force field lasts for 10 minutes. Each time the field prevents damage, the duration decreases by 1 minute.
name: Hardshell Surki

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 49 The nodes in your abdomen are particularly luminous. You can use an Interact action to shed light from your abdomen in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). This is a [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) effect with a level equal to your own. You can change the color of the light or extinguish it with another Interact action.
  • **Evolution** Your abdominal nodes have evolved into a pair of secondary limbs that project a magical focusing lens. You gain the Lantern Beam action.
    **Lantern Beam** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Requirements** Your lantern light is shining; **Effect** Your lenses focus your light into a searing beam. You deal 5d4 fire damage to all creatures in a 30-foot line, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. Your lantern light then extinguishes, and you can't use this ability or use your lantern light again for 1d4 rounds. At 11th level and every two levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4.
  • **Evolution** Secondary light emitters grow from your shoulder nodes that flash in time with your lantern. You gain the Lantern Strobe action.
    **Lantern Strobe** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** As you pulse your lantern, your emitters flash erratically, disorienting your opponents that see it. Each opponent in a 15-foot cone must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher). On a failure, the creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. On a critical failure, the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and dazzled for the following round.
name: Lantern Surki

<traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 9

Your family has particularly long, sharp claws capable of delivering bleeding wounds with a wicked swipe. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage. Your claws are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Hunting Catfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=314)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 9

You come from a long line of skilled hunters and trackers and have a particularly keen sense of smell. You gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine a creature's location. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from the creature or halve the range if you're upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) a creature or object if you've smelled it before.
name: Hunting Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Liminal Catfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=316)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 9

You've inherited a closeness to the far corners of the world, where the boundaries between dimensions grow thin. You can cast the [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) cantrip as an occult innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to half your level rounded up. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about creatures that originated on planes other than [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26).
name: Liminal Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Nine Lives Catfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=317)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 9

Your family has always seems to bounce back from disaster, not through physical hardiness or specialized skill, but from sheer luck. Other catfolk whisper that you have nine lives. While you're [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), you don't add your dying value to the DC of your recovery checks (this means the DC is typically 10). In addition, you gain the [Diehard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5140) general feat.
name: Nine Lives Catfolk

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 9

You were born with big, expressive ears that move with your moods and perk up at any unexpected sound. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to locate [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures that you could hear within 30 feet with a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action. As long as you're aware of a creature via sound, once per round, your ears can help you [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=2315) the creature to all allies as a free action.
name: Sharp-Eared Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Elfbane Hobgoblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=320)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 13

Hobgoblins were engineered long ago from the unreliable and fecund [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) to be used as an army against the [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2). Although the elves ultimately freed the hobgoblins from their bondage, some hobgoblins retain ancestral resistance to magic, which they refer to as “elf magic.” You gain the Resist Elf Magic reaction.

 **Resist Elf Magic** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against a [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) effect but haven't rolled yet; **Effect** Your ancestral resistance to magic protects you. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the triggering saving throw. If the triggering effect is [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), you gain a +2 circumstance bonus instead.
name: Elfbane Hobgoblin

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shortshanks Hobgoblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=322)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 13

You have a longer torso and broader shoulders than most hobgoblins, making your legs seem short by comparison. This gives you a strong, muscular core and lowers your center of gravity—features that assist you in riding and climbing—and you've trained at riding in a saddle from an extremely early age. You gain the [Ride](/Feats.aspx?ID=5206) feat. Additionally, you are not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while you [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374).
name: Shortshanks Hobgoblin

text: Shortshanks Hobgoblin Source Player Core 2 pg. 13 You have a longer torso and broader shoulders than most hobgoblins, making your legs seem short by comparison. This gives you a strong, muscular core and lowers your center of gravity—features that assist you in riding and climbing—and you've trained at riding in a saddle from an extremely early age. You gain the Ride feat. Additionally, you are not off-guard while you Climb.

<traits>

</traits>

Your family have been alchemists, engineers, and scientists for generations, laboring on projects that bring smoke and fire to the field of battle. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). You automatically succeed at the DC 5 flat check to target a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) creature if that creature is concealed only by smoke.

You come from a line of wandering mercenaries constantly on the march and scavenging food on the trail. When exploring, you can [Hustle](/Actions.aspx?ID=2626) twice as long before you have to stop.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 13

Your ancestors lived underground. Your ears are larger than those of other hobgoblins and sensitive to echoes. While you're underground, when you target an opponent that is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from you or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from you, reduce the DC of the flat check to 3 for a concealed target or 9 for a hidden one. In addition, if you roll a success on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), you get a critical success instead.
name: Warrenbred Hobgoblin

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 17

You're a sharp-featured, big-eared kholo about 3 feet tall. Many are skeptical that you are in fact a kholo. Your size is Small instead of Medium. You are trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) (or another skill if you were already trained in Deception). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) when specifically claiming innocence, to Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) checks to uncover such lies, and to initiative checks when you roll Deception for initiative.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 17

Storytellers spin ancient tales claiming that kholo lived in caves and underground before most of your kind ventured into the light. You're a throwback to these ancients, with a broad chest and markings that resemble short black slashes instead of spots. Your eyes are developed to see perfectly in the dark, a valuable advantage to your clan. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Oathkeeper Dwarf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=33)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 19

Growing up, you never tried to lie to get what you wanted, and even when necessary, lying makes you uncomfortable. The especially faithful might even have thought that you were blessed by the dwarven god of duty, [Kols](/Deities.aspx?ID=89). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) and to Perception DCs against attempts to Lie to you. Furthermore, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to convince others you speak the truth when you are telling the truth, and you take a –4 circumstance penalty to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) and to your Deception DC against Sense Motive.
name: Oathkeeper Dwarf

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Cavernstalker Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=333)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 21

You hatched in a warren with narrow tunnels that was also home to a being of primal earth energy, which has made you limber and flexible. When [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) rock walls, stalactites, and other natural stone features, you move at half your Speed on a success and at full Speed on a critical success (and you move at full Speed on a success if you have [Quick Climb](/Feats.aspx?ID=5192)). This doesn't affect you if you're using a climb Speed. If you roll a success on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), you get a critical success instead.
name: Cavernstalker Kobold

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 21

Thanks to the proximity of a venomous creature to your warren, you were born with a vestigial spur in your tail that secretes deadly venom. You gain the Tail Toxin action.

 **Tail Toxin** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You are wielding a piercing or slashing weapon; **Effect** You apply your tail's venom to a piercing or slashing weapon. If your next Strike with that weapon before the end of your next turn hits and deals damage, you deal [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) equal to your level to the target.
name: Venomtail Kobold

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ancient Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=34)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 25

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The ancient elf heritage requires an elven lifespan (a feature that half-elves do not have) and thus cannot be selected by half-elves using the Elf Atavism feat, only by full elves._

In your long life, you've dabbled in many paths and many styles. A typical ancient elf is at least 100 years old, though you might be younger at the GM's discretion. Choose a class other than your own. You gain the [multiclass dedication feat](/Feats.aspx?Traits=115) for that class, even though you don't meet its level prerequisite. You must still meet its other prerequisites to gain the feat.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 25

Your toes are adapted for gripping and climbing. You gain the [Combat Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=5134) feat as a bonus feat, and as long as you aren't wearing footwear, you can use the sticky pads on your feet to climb, leaving your hands free. Additionally, if you roll a success on an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to climb, you get a critical success instead.
name: Cliffscale Lizardfolk
