<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 25

Your body is light with flaps of skin along your limbs that catch the air that passes beneath you. As long as you can act and have enough room, you can open the flaps to slow any [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) just enough to avoid taking damage (whether you have the room to do so depends on the GM's discretion).

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Frilled Lizardfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=342)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 25

You can flare your neck frill and flex your dorsal spines, [Demoralizing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) your foes. When you do, Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language. You also gain the Threatening Approach action.

 **Threatening Approach** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Effect** You Stride to be adjacent to a foe and Demoralize that foe. If you succeed, the foe is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) instead of frightened 1.
name: Frilled Lizardfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Sandstrider Lizardfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=343)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 25

Your thick scales help you retain water and combat the sun's glare. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). [Environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step less extreme for you, and you can go 10 times as long as normal before you are affected by [starvation or thirst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603). However, unless you wear protective gear or take shelter, environmental cold effects are one step more extreme for you.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 25

You can change your skin color to blend in with your surroundings, making minor shifts with a single action and dramatic changes over the course of an hour. When you're in an area where your coloration is roughly similar to the environment (for instance, forest green in a forest), you can use the minor, single-action application of this ability to make localized changes that help you blend into your surroundings, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks until your surroundings change in coloration or pattern.

You move easily through thick forest and jungle, using every branch or bush. You can always use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) action when in forest or jungle terrain to gain cover, even if you're not next to an obstacle you would normally be able to Take Cover behind. You gain the [Terrain Stalker](/Feats.aspx?ID=5224) feat, even if you're not trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48), and you must choose underbrush as your chosen terrain.

You are native to arid plains and likely grew up traveling the roads. If you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours. In addition, [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step less extreme for you, and you can go 10 times longer than normal before you are affected by [starvation or thirst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603). However, unless you wear protective gear or take shelter, environmental cold effects are one step more extreme for you.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 29

Your long snout gives you a keener sense of smell than most ratfolk. You gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine a creature's location. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from the creature or halve the range if you're upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) a creature or object within the range of your scent.

You come from a long line of ratfolk with a community based in the sewers beneath a large settlement. You are immune to the disease putrid plague. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). If you roll a success on a saving throw against a disease or poison, you get a critical success instead. If you have a different ability that would improve the save in this way (such as the battle hardened fighter class feature), if you roll a critical failure on the save you get a failure instead.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 29

Your ancestors lived in dark spaces underground, granting you dark fur and a vaguely unnatural mien. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) and can use Intimidation to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). When you [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) an animal, you don't take a penalty for not sharing a language with it. If you would automatically become trained in Intimidation (from your background or class, for example), you become trained in another skill of your choice.

 Animals' attitudes toward you begin one degree worse than normal, usually starting at [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) instead of indifferent for domesticated animals, and [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) instead of unfriendly for wild animals.

<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 42

You are the scion of a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480), half living and half [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), gifted with uncanny charm and grace, a bloodless pallor, and elongated incisors. You gain the [dhampir](/Traits.aspx?ID=750) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) ability, which means you are harmed by vitality damage and healed by void effects as if you were undead. You also gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision. You can select from [dhampir feats](/Feats.aspx?Traits=750) and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 44 You're descended in some way from a dragon. Your physical features might show this outwardly, with a pair of draconic horns, patches of scaly skin, or even a tail, or you might develop an internal reserve of draconic power. You gain the [dragonblood](/Traits.aspx?ID=794) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. Add [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) to your ancestry's list of additional languages (allowing you to choose it as a language if your Intelligence modifier is positive). When you roll a success on a saving throw against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect, you get a critical success instead. You can choose from [dragonblood feats](/Feats.aspx?Traits=794) and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat. Draconic Exemplars Some abilities reference your draconic exemplar, which is a specific type of dragon from which you draw your draconic powers—typically the type of dragon whose blood runs in your veins. You can choose your exemplar when you create your character, but are only required to choose one when you select a feat or other option that requires you to have one (such as Breath of the Dragon). This means your draconic nature might be a mystery to you for some time. The table below lists the dragons from Monster Core and their specifications. The dragon breath entry lists if the given breath uses a Fortitude (F), Reflex (R), or Will (W) saving throw. If you choose a dragon from a different source, work with your GM to determine if you have access to it. [Click here for the full list of Draconic Exemplars.](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) ##
DragonTraditionSpeedsDragon Breath
AdamantinePrimalBurrowCone of bludgeoningR
ConspiratorOccultClimbCone of poisonF
DiabolicDivine\-Cone of fireR
EmpyrealDivine\-Cone of spiritR
FortuneArcane\-Cone of forceR
HornedPrimalSwimCone of poisonF
MirageArcaneClimbCone of mentalW
OmenOccult\-Cone of mentalW
name: Dragonblood
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />

text: Dragonblood Source Player Core 2 pg. 44 You're descended in some way from a dragon. Your physical features might show this outwardly, with a pair of draconic horns, patches of scaly skin, or even a tail, or you might develop an internal reserve of draconic power. You gain the dragonblood trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. Add Draconic to your ancestry's list of additional languages (allowing you to choose it as a language if your Intelligence modifier is positive). When you roll a success on a saving throw against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. You can choose from dragonblood feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat. Draconic Exemplars Some abilities reference your draconic exemplar, which is a specific type of dragon from which you draw your draconic powers—typically the type of dragon whose blood runs in your veins. You can choose your exemplar when you create your character, but are only required to choose one when you select a feat or other option that requires you to have one (such as Breath of the Dragon). This means your draconic nature might be a mystery to you for some time. The table below lists the dragons from Monster Core and their specifications. The dragon breath entry lists if the given breath uses a Fortitude (F) , Reflex (R) , or Will (W) saving throw. If you choose a dragon from a different source, work with your GM to determine if you have access to it. Click here for the full list of Draconic Exemplars. ## Dragon Tradition Speeds Dragon Breath Adamantine Primal Burrow Cone of bludgeoning R Conspirator Occult Climb Cone of poison F Diabolic Divine \- Cone of fire R Empyreal Divine \- Cone of spirit R Fortune Arcane \- Cone of force R Horned Primal Swim Cone of poison F Mirage Arcane Climb Cone of mental W Omen Occult \- Cone of mental W

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Duskwalker](/Heritages.aspx?ID=369)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 48

Thanks to an ancient bargain, your soul has been reborn as a duskwalker, a planar scion with a connection to [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) and the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39). You gain the [duskwalker](/Traits.aspx?ID=751) trait. You also gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision. Neither your body nor your spirit can ever become [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). You can select from [duskwalker feats](/Feats.aspx?Traits=751) and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Duskwalker

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 36

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

One of your parents was an oni or a hungerseed. You possess a pair of horns, ranging in size from conical nubs to lengthy protrusions. You might have other signs of your parentage, such as colorful skin, fangs and claws, or a third eye in your forehead. You gain the [oni](/Traits.aspx?ID=765) trait. You gain a horns unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage and is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group. You can select from hungerseed feats and feats from your other parent's ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Hungerseed

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Chrysanthemum Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=375)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 42

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

You look like a human child, though with a grand crown of chrysanthemums growing from your head, ornate as an emperor's. These petals have medicinal properties, granting you a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). Furthermore, during your daily preparations, you can harmlessly pluck a few petals from your head and steep them in fresh water to create a single [lesser antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3296), which takes the form of a tea. At level 6, you instead create a moderate antidote; at level 10, a greater antidote; and at level 14, a major antidote. The tea loses its effectiveness if not consumed before your next daily preparations.

<title level="2">Feats that require Chrysanthemum Leshy...</title>
[Childlike Plant](/Feats.aspx?ID=6853)
name: Chrysanthemum Leshy

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Peachchild Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=376)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 42

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

Your leshy spirit took hold in a massive peach fruit before your body split forth from it, possibly emulating a past hero who was born in the same manner. You look like a human child, though with a perpetual flush of pink to your complexion and perhaps a few peach leaves growing from your body. The nature spirit inside you puts certain animals at ease. You can ask questions of and receive answers from household animals and livestock, such as dogs or pheasants, as well as use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) things of them. Most domesticated animals have an indifferent or friendly starting attitude toward you and give you time to make your case, though other animals react to you like any other adventurer.

<title level="2">Feats that require Peachchild Leshy...</title>
[Childlike Plant](/Feats.aspx?ID=6853)
name: Peachchild Leshy

You have a trunk-like snout and a connection to the divine. When you cast this cantrip, it loses the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, as you cast purely by roaring and flaring your trunk.

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

You have the form of a faceless, leathery ball with short fur, six legs, and a connection to primordial chaos. While you have no face, head, mouth, eyes, or ears, you can somehow hear, speak, see, eat, and breathe just fine (though it's unclear exactly how). Your erratic nature means you often react to stimuli in unpredictable ways. When you roll a failure (though not a critical failure) on a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect, you get a success instead.
name: Dijiang

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Gandharva](/Heritages.aspx?ID=380)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 46

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

You're descended from the self-proclaimed musicians, poets, and dancers of the gods, with a body that's part humanoid and part horse or bird. If you gain wings, they come in many different brilliant colors. Due to your [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) connection, the tradition of any spells or magical abilities you gain from a sprite heritage or ancestry feat is [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) instead of its normal tradition. You become trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) (or another skill of your choice if you're already trained in Performance), and you can cast [_summon instrument_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1703) as a divine innate cantrip.
name: Gandharva

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 46

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_

You have the head of a mouse deer and hooves instead of feet, with a reputation for being clever and persuasive—able to get yourself out of sticky situations with wordplay and a bit of luck. You become trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) (or another skill if you were already trained in Deception). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) when specifically attempting to avoid danger or punishment (such as trying to convince a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395) not to eat you), to Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) checks to uncover such lies, and to initiative rolls when you roll Deception for initiative.
name: Kanchil

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 48 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this uncommon heritage_ You have the form and memories of a common tool. Choose one Lore skill related to what kind of tool you were; for instance, Cooking Lore for a kitchen knife or spoon. You're trained in this skill, and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Aid with this skill. If you take the [Helpful Poppet](/Feats.aspx?ID=3341) feat, the bonus increases to +3.
While most poppets are made of stuffing, cloth, or soft metals, some tsukumogami are made of other materials, changing the poppet weakness to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604). If your body is primarily wood or cloth, you have the normal poppet weakness to fire. If your body is primarily metal, you're instead weak to [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586); if its primarily ceramic, you're instead weak to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555). Feats that require Tsukumogami Poppet... [Awaken the Obake](/Feats.aspx?ID=6878), [Insulated Poppet](/Feats.aspx?ID=6875), [Made for Combat](/Feats.aspx?ID=6872), [No Hands, No Problems](/Feats.aspx?ID=6876), [Solidarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=6877), [Sudden Terror](/Feats.aspx?ID=6873)
name: Tsukumogami Poppet

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 50 You descend from kitsune who possessed such compelling powers of persuasion that they could walk into a palace at sunrise and end up as the power behind the throne by nightfall. You gain the Nudging Whisper action.
**Activate—Nudging Whisper** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Effect** You lace your words with echoes of your ancestors’ magic. If your next action is to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393), you gain the effects of the [Shameless Request](/Feats.aspx?ID=5211) feat, and if your next action is to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), you gain the effects of the [Confabulator](/Feats.aspx?ID=5136) feat. If you critically fail at either check, the target and anyone who witnessed you fail becomes immune to your Nudging Whisper for one day, though this doesn’t necessarily let them know that you attempted to charm them.
Your alternate form is a common Medium humanoid ancestry prevalent where you grew up (typically human) called a tailless form.
name: Palace Echoes Kitsune

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 51

You were born with a keen sense for magic, able to taste its unique effects in the air. You gain magicsense as a  [vague sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2408) that has a range of 30 feet—like all vague senses, it's only about as precise as an average human's sense of smell, meaning you generally can predict only if magic is present; however, each tradition of magic has a unique taste to you, allowing you to identify the tradition of magic present.
name: Shimmertongue Nagaji

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Healer Samsaran](/Heritages.aspx?ID=386)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 54

In seeking enlightenment, your past incarnations have wandered away from the ancestral homelands of Zi Ha, exploring the different nations of Tian Xia and elsewhere in Golarion. Your past incarnations had dangerous encounters while traveling, and you frequently have flashbacks to [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) wounds. You become trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42). If you would automatically become trained in Medicine (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. When you use Medicine to [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) on yourself, you can use your special techniques to add your level to the Hit Points you regain from the treatment.
name: Healer Samsaran

Your past incarnations dwelled in the caves on the snowcapped mountains of Tian Xia, so your body has become adept at surviving frigid storms. <title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Full Moon Sarangay](/Heritages.aspx?ID=391)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 60

You were raised in a shamanic tradition transcending a single sarangay community or heritage. You spent your youth under the guidance of a shaman elder, who taught you to commune with spirits in the hope that you'll one day use that knowledge to advise and guide your people. Those from your tradition are priests or shamans who channel the spirits and see things others can't. You gain an ancestry attribute boost to Wisdom instead of Strength, and you gain an attribute flaw in Constitution instead of Wisdom. You gain the [Folk Healer](/Feats.aspx?ID=6914) ancestry feat.
name: Full Moon Sarangay
search_markdown:

text: Full Moon Sarangay Source Tian Xia Character Guide pg. 60 You were raised in a shamanic tradition transcending a single sarangay community or heritage. You spent your youth under the guidance of a shaman elder, who taught you to commune with spirits in the hope that you'll one day use that knowledge to advise and guide your people. Those from your tradition are priests or shamans who channel the spirits and see things others can't. You gain an ancestry attribute boost to Wisdom instead of Strength, and you gain an attribute flaw in Constitution instead of Wisdom. You gain the Folk Healer ancestry feat.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Half Moon Sarangay](/Heritages.aspx?ID=392)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 60

You come from one of many traveling communities that migrate seasonally along Tian Xia's vast rivers and mountains, bringing news and tidings to villages along their path. Your people connect sarangay communities to one another across vast distances and are symbolized by the half moon, which conjoins the light and dark; so too do you conjoin the dark from the bright, the red from the blue, the wind from the sea. Your ancestors were tall and burly with shiny black fur, light spots or markings, and short, tightly curved horns. You become trained in two Lore skills of your choice, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks using those skills.

[Traveler's Counsel](/Feats.aspx?ID=6916)

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[New Moon Sarangay](/Heritages.aspx?ID=393)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 60

Some people call you a dwarf sarangay and your kind “hiding ones.” Others whisper you go out with the moon when the moon “goes hunting”—a common folk saying for when the new moon vanishes from the sky. Your elusive ancestors built their lodgings in the shade of bamboo thickets, and your ancestral communities valued caution and independence, passing down the knowledge of walking lightly and moving like smoke through bamboo. Your ancestors had dark brown or gray fur with white markings and a V-shaped pair of flat, triangular horns. Your size is Small instead of Medium. You gain 10 Hit Points from your ancestry instead of 8 and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380).

<title level="2">Feats that require New Moon Sarangay...</title>
[Smoke Through Bamboo](/Feats.aspx?ID=6919)

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 60 Your ancestors were named for the moon in transition, ever-changing and ephemeral. They're the keepers of secret arts, just as the waning moon hides its face, and their communities were highly regarded for their storytellers, performers, and artists. You lived among your community on the outskirts of forests or rivers, where many answers came as riddles, and you learned to see the world from different perspectives. Your people are often red or fawn colored, with prominent eyes and flat, compact horns. You become trained in your choice of [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45).
Once per day, when you roll a critical failure with the chosen skill, you can reroll the check, taking the new result, even if it's worse. Rerolling in this way is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.
name: Waning Moon Sarangay

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 60

Your ancestors dwelled in the swamps, fens, and flooded forests, where they contended with fierce predators and formed raiding parties to claim scarce resources. Blessed by the growing crescent, you're one of the eviscerators, and you can overcome threats that lurk in murky waters as well as those that hunt on land. You're a powerful swimmer with brown or gray fur and backward- or downward-curving horns. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381).
name: Waxing Moon Sarangay

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Ascetic Tanuki](/Heritages.aspx?ID=396)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 65

It's said your round form is the result of calmness and restraint, not indulgence. You gain scent as an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from something you're trying to smell or halve the range if you're upwind. In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks whenever you're trying to locate food, drink, or a [consumable](/Traits.aspx?ID=564) item that's ingested (such as a [potion](/Traits.aspx?ID=672) or [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=589)) using your scent.

You're possessed of a serenity uncommon to other tanuki, who always seem to be flying off the handle. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects. If you roll a success at a saving throw against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead, but when you roll a failure at a saving throw against an emotion effect, you get a critical failure instead.

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 66 Your heart beats with the courage of those who came before you, giving you the kind of bravery only a tanuki can demonstrate. Whenever you gain the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition, you also gain a +10-foot circumstance bonus to your Speeds that lasts as long as you're fleeing. When you have the fleeing condition, instead of having to spend all your actions trying to escape, you can act normally for one action but must still spend the remainder of your actions fleeing. You also gain the Tactical Retreat ability.
**Activate—Tactical Retreat** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), tanuki) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition; **Effect** Realizing that discretion is the better part of valor, you opt to put some distance between you and a threat. You gain the fleeing condition until the beginning of your next turn, and you Stride.
name: Courageous Tanuki

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 36

Your ancestors lived and worked among the great ancient stones of the mountains or the depths of the earth. This makes you solid as a rock when you plant your feet. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

 In addition, if any effect would force you to move 10 feet or more, you are moved only half the distance.

Hobgoblins were engineered long ago from the unreliable and fecund goblins, to be used as an army against the elves. Although the elves ultimately freed the hobgoblins from their bondage, some hobgoblins retain ancestral resistance to magic, which they refer to as “elf magic.” You gain the Resist Elf Magic reaction.

**Activate—Inscribe Shadow Pamor** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You plunge your fist or weapon into your shadow, enveloping it in a rippling, damask-like pattern of darkness. If your next action is to Strike, the shadows cling to your foe with your attack, obscuring its vision. On a hit, the target of your Strike becomes dazzled until the start of your next turn. The target can use an Interact action to tear away the clinging shadows and remove the dazzled condition. On a critical hit, the shadows are particularly stubborn and can’t be torn away.

summary_markdown: Your shadow is thick and liquid, like it could quench the finest ore. You gain the Inscribe Shadow Pamor action.
text: Shadow of the Smith Source Tian Xia Character Guide pg. 72 Your shadow is thick and liquid, like it could quench the finest ore. You gain the Inscribe Shadow Pamor action. Activate—Inscribe Shadow Pamor Single Action (occult, shadow) Frequency once per minute; Effect You plunge your fist or weapon into your shadow, enveloping it in a rippling, damask-like pattern of darkness. If your next action is to Strike, the shadows cling to your foe with your attack, obscuring its vision. On a hit, the target of your Strike becomes dazzled until the start of your next turn. The target can use an Interact action to tear away the clinging shadows and remove the dazzled condition. On a critical hit, the shadows are particularly stubborn and can’t be torn away.

**Additional Edict** confront rakshasas and asuras you come across (as long as you have a reasonable chance of success); in the unlikely event you find a benevolent rakshasa or asura, you don't have to confront them
name: Deny the Traitor's Rebirth

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to protect the waylaid and the lost. Both environmental heat effects and [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) effects are one step less extreme for you (incredible heat becomes extreme, extreme cold becomes severe, and so on), and you gain a + 1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against environmental features or hazards, such as floods, rockslides, and sandstorms.
**Additional Edict** assist lost or incapacitated travelers
name: Respite of Cloudless Paths

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Runtboss Hobgoblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=41)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 49

You come from a long line of hobgoblins who commanded goblins. You are smaller than other hobgoblins, but goblins still listen to any commands you bellow. You gain the [Group Coercion](/Feats.aspx?ID=788) skill feat. If you roll a success on an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) (but not other creatures with the goblin trait), you get a critical success instead; if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.
name: Runtboss Hobgoblin

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 78 You swore a vow to preserve the very foundation of sanctuary: the great earth and all that grows upon it. Your vow grants you the land's spiritual power; you gain one cantrip from the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate primal spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. Your roots in the world grant you the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait.
**Additional Edict** cure or remove blight and pollution from the plants and soil you encounter
name: Respite of Loam and Leaf

  • **Yaoguai Form** You can dash like an animal. If you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours.

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were once a powerful [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) before an escape or great punishment left you trapped in a mortal shell, with only vague memories and limited power. Due to your residual [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) power, the tradition of any spells or magical abilities you gain from a yaoguai heritage or ancestry feat is divine instead of its normal tradition (usually [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)).
  • **Humanoid Form** Fragments of divine memory still litter your mind. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.
  • **Yaoguai Form** Celestial power flows through you. Choose one cantrip from the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spell list. You can cast this spell as an innate divine cantrip at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
Feats that require Born of Celestial... [Crawling Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6990)

markdown:
**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were an aspect of nature—the rain, the wind, a ray of light—until the wild essences gave you a soul. Due to your natural connection, the tradition of any spells or magical abilities you gain from a yaoguai heritage or ancestry feat is [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) instead of its normal tradition (usually [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)).
  • **Humanoid Form** You remain attuned to the natural world. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Sense Direction, and you don't take a penalty if you don't have a compass.
  • **Yaoguai Form** The power of nature flows through you, ready to lash out. Choose [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [_needle darts_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1375), [_timber_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1412), [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2021) , [_slashing gust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1321), or [_spout_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2031). You can cast this spell as an innate primal cantrip at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
Feats that require Born of Elements... [Immobile Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6999)

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 84 You were an object until the moon breathed life into you. Choose one Lore skill related to what kind of tool you were; for instance, Cooking Lore for a cleaver or Farming Lore for a rake. You become trained in this skill.
  • **Humanoid Form** You retain memories of the tasks you performed as an object. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the Lore skill you obtained through this heritage.
  • **Yaoguai Form** Your time as a mindless object makes it harder to affect you mentally. If you roll a success on a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, you gain a critical success instead.

You gain one cantrip from the [occult spell list](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3). You can cast this spell as an occult [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

You have a brightly colored body with gleaming scales that become more lustrous as you age, and iridescent butterfly wings. You can change the coloration and pattern of your wings with a single action to let you blend into an area. When your wings match your environment, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks until your surroundings shift in coloration or pattern. You gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) trait and add [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) to your list of known languages.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 210

You have a short, stubby nose, and tiny spines that resemble hairs. You might have vividly colored scales or be a dun brown. You can cast the [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) cantrip as an [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up. In addition, you always know the distance and direction to your home. You can designate a new location as your home after you spend 1 week there, but you can have only a single designated home at a time.
name: Homing Drake

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Reflection](/Heritages.aspx?ID=428)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 120

You were created as a duplicate of another creature, intentionally or accidentally, though you might not know of your origins. Other than a minor mark or two, you look just like your progenitor. You gain the [reflection](/Traits.aspx?ID=902) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You don't need to attempt Deception checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) your progenitor unless you're interacting with people who know them personally or you do something known to be out of character for them. The GM might require you to roll a Deception check in other circumstances, such as if you're mirror-risen and interacting with someone who has seen an accurate likeness of your progenitor and might notice a distinguishing feature on the reverse side. You can choose from reflection feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.
name: Reflection

</traits>

You were designed to serve as a scout or assassin and have a body resembling a pack hunter like a large cat or wolf. Though you typically move like a quadruped, you can still stand and fight like a biped, allowing you to use all equipment normally. Your quadruped design allows you to move quickly; if you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours.

<trait label="Surprisingly Common" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=759" />
</traits>

**Source** [Fools Aplenty](/Sources.aspx?ID=323)

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

You are actually three individual kobolds in a trench coat acting as a single entity. You are Medium instead of Small. You gain a +10 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) not being three kobolds in a trench coat. You also gain the Trenchcoat Topple reaction. 

**Trenchcoat Topple** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** twice per day; **Trigger** You would be affected by an ability that permanently removes a body part, such as the decapitation effect of a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon; **Effect** One of your kobolds jumps free of the trench coat, negating the effect.

**Special** The second time you use this in a day, your size is reduced to Small until your next daily preparations.
name: Three Kobolds in a Trench Coat

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Aquatic Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=435)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 19

You are an aquatic elf who spends most of your life underwater. You have the ability to breathe both air and water, but you cannot live indefinitely on the surface. You gain the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529) trait, and you have a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 30 feet. At least once a week, you must take an 8-hour period of rest underwater. If you do not, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and you cannot recover from this fatigue until you rest underwater.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Cecaelia Merfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=436)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 20

While the bodies of most merfolk resemble the bottom half of a fish, your lower half is akin to a cephalopod. You have multiple tentacles rather than legs or a fish tail, and those tentacles are lined with suction cups. Your land Speed becomes 10 feet, and you gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 10 feet. Additionally, your abundance of legs allows you to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).

<title level="2">Feats that require Cecaelia Merfolk...</title>
[Hallucinogenic Ink](/Feats.aspx?ID=8999), [Spray Ink](/Feats.aspx?ID=8997)

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 49

Your ancestors lived underground. Your ears are larger than those of other hobgoblins and sensitive to echoes. When you are underground, you can use the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to sense [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures within a 30-foot burst instead of a 15-foot burst. In addition, if you roll a success on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), you get a critical success instead.
name: Warrenbred Hobgoblin

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Gourd Leshy](/Heritages.aspx?ID=46)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 53

You have a large gourd for a skull. Your knowledge comes from within your spirit, rather than a physical brain, and you have found a handier use for the space inside your head. You can store a collection of up to 1 Bulk of objects within your head. The DC of checks to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) objects from inside your head increases by 4. Additionally, if you store only one object within your head, you can draw it effortlessly into your hand as part of another action to use the object. Drawing the item grants this other action the [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639) trait.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 36

Your blood runs hearty and strong, and you can shake off toxins. You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and each of your successful saving throws against a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) affliction reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) poison. Each critical success against an ongoing poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent poison.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 57

You can flare your neck frill and flex your dorsal spines, [Demoralizing](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) your foes. When you do, Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language. You also gain the Threatening Approach action.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 57

Your thick scales help you retain water and combat the sun's glare. You gain [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). [Environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step less extreme for you, and you can go 10 times as long as normal before you are affected by [starvation or thirst](/Rules.aspx?ID=537). However, unless you wear protective gear or take shelter, environmental cold effects are one step more extreme for you.

This means you can use your sense of smell to determine the location of a creature, but it remains [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22). <title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Limited">[Thickcoat Shoony](/Heritages.aspx?ID=57)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #153: Life's Long Shadows](/Sources.aspx?ID=29) pg. 71

You are a rare shoony who hails from colder climes. You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and you treat [environmental cold effects as if they were one step less extreme](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) (incredible cold becomes extreme, extreme cold becomes severe, and so on). You don’t need to succeed at a flat check to target a concealed creature if that creature is concealed only by snow. Unless you wear protective gear or take shelter, environmental heat effects are one step more extreme for you.
name: Thickcoat Shoony

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Hunting Catfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=59)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 9

You come from a long line of skilled hunters and trackers and have a particularly keen sense of smell. You gain [imprecise scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine a creature's location. The GM will usually double the range if you're downwind from the creature or halve the range if you're upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature or object if you've smelled it before.
name: Hunting Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Cavern Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=63)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 13

You hatched into a warren among narrow tunnels and countless kin. Your body is flexible. When Climbing rock walls, stalactites, and other natural stone features, you move at half your Speed on a success and at full Speed on a critical success (and you move at full Speed on a success if you have [Quick Climb](/Feats.aspx?ID=821)). This doesn't affect you if you're using a climb Speed. If you roll a success on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), you get a critical success instead.
name: Cavern Kobold

A trace of draconic magic flows through your veins. Choose one cantrip from the [arcane spell list](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1). You can Cast this Spell as an arcane innate spell at will. **Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 21

You are native to arid plains and likely grew up traveling the roads. You have a leaner build than other ratfolk, with longer limbs and short fur. If you have both hands free, you can increase your Speed to 30 feet as you run on all fours. In addition, [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step less extreme for you, and you can go 10 times as long as normal before you are affected by starvation or thirst. However, unless you wear protective gear or take shelter, environmental cold effects are one step more extreme for you.
name: Desert Rat

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Longsnout Rat](/Heritages.aspx?ID=74)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 21

The long snouts that run in your family give you a keener sense of smell than most ratfolk. You gain [imprecise scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with a range of 30 feet. This means you can use your sense of smell to determine a creature’s location. The GM will usually double the range if you’re downwind from the creature or halve the range if you’re upwind.

 In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) a creature or object within the range of your scent.

<title level="2">Feats that require Longsnout Rat...</title>
[Plague Sniffer](/Feats.aspx?ID=2428)
name: Longsnout Rat

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Sewer Rat](/Heritages.aspx?ID=75)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 22

You come from a long line of ysoki from a community based in the sewers beneath a large settlement. You are immune to the disease filth fever. Each of your successful saving throws against a disease or poison reduces its stage by 2, or by 1 for a [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) disease or poison. Each critical success against an ongoing disease or poison reduces its stage by 3, or by 2 for a virulent disease or poison.
name: Sewer Rat

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shadow Rat](/Heritages.aspx?ID=76)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 22

Your ancestors lived in dark spaces underground, granting you dark fur and a vaguely unnatural mien that unnerves sapient creatures and frightens animals. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) and can use Intimidation to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) animals. When you [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) an animal, you don’t take a penalty for not sharing a language with it. If you would automatically become trained in Intimidation (from your background or class, for example), you become trained in another skill of your choice.

 [Animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9)’ attitudes toward you begin one degree worse than normal, usually starting at [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40) instead of [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25) for domesticated animals, and [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) instead of unfriendly for wild animals.
name: Shadow Rat

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 25

Your lineage has been exposed to curse after curse, and now, they slide off your feathers like rain. If you succeed at a saving throw against a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect, you get a critical success instead. When you would gain the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) condition, attempt a DC 17 flat check. On a success, reduce the value of the doomed condition you would gain by 1.
name: Jinxed Tengu

You come from a line of tengu ascetics, leaving you with a link to the spirits of the world and the Great Beyond. You can cast the [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79) cantrip as a primal innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up. Each time you cast a spell from a tengu heritage or ancestry feat, you can decide whether it’s a divine or primal spell.

You have an inborn ability to detect and understand magical phenomena. You can cast the [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) cantrip as an arcane innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.

 In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24) and to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) of a magical nature. These skill actions typically use the [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) skill.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Changeling](/Heritages.aspx?ID=82)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 30

Your mother was a hag. Your heterochromatic eyes are the most obvious signifier of this parentage, but you likely also have a slighter build, paler skin, and darker hair than most members of your other parent’s ancestry. You gain the [changeling](/Traits.aspx?ID=212) trait. You also gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision. You can select from changeling feats and feats from your other parent’s ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 32

You are the scion of a vampire, half living and half undead, gifted with uncanny charm and grace, a bloodless pallor, and elongated incisors. You gain the [dhampir](/Traits.aspx?ID=215) trait, in addition to the traits from your ancestry. You have the [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) ability, which means you are harmed by positive damage and healed by negative effects as if you were undead. You also gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision. You can choose from dhampir feats and feats from your ancestry whenever you gain an ancestry feat.

You were born with a strange blessing: bereft of the typical halfling luck, you can instead manipulate the fortunes of others. You can never take the [Halfling Luck](/Feats.aspx?ID=54) feat, and you gain the Jinx action.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 13

Your ancestors had to survive in polluted water that made every breath noxious. You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). Furthermore, you can survive outside of water for 72 hours before you begin to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

<traits>

</traits>

You live in murky waters and have grown accustomed to life in a low-visibility environment. You need only a successful DC 3 flat check when targeting a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) creature and a successful DC 9 flat check when targeting a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) creature.

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Whisper Elf](/Heritages.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 40

Your ears are finely tuned, able to detect even the slightest whispers of sound. As long as you can hear normally, you can use the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to sense [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures in a 60-foot cone instead of a 30-foot cone. You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to locate undetected creatures that you could hear within 30 feet with a Seek action.
name: Whisper Elf

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 13

Your skin can detect the slightest changes in ocean currents. You gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) with a range of 30 feet, allowing you to sense motion in the water around you. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) in aquatic environments.

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 17

You've inherited flexibility beyond that of most humanoids. You don't treat tight spaces that don't require you to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) as difficult terrain. In addition, you get a +1 circumstance bonus to checks when you attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 17

You've inherited a closeness to the far corners of the world, where the boundaries between dimensions grow thin. You can cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) as an occult innate cantrip heightened to half your level, rounded up. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about creatures that originated outside of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1).
name: Liminal Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Sharp-Eared Catfolk](/Heritages.aspx?ID=94)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 17

You were born with big, expressive ears that move with your moods and perk up at any unexpected sound. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to locate [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) creatures that you could hear within 30 feet with a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action. As long as you're aware of a creature via sound, once per round, your ears can help you [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=97) the creature to all allies as a free action.
name: Sharp-Eared Catfolk

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Shortshanks Hobgoblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=95)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 33

You have a longer torso and broader shoulders than most hobgoblins, making your legs seem short by comparison. This gives you a strong, muscular core and lowers your center of gravity—features that assist you in riding and climbing—and you've trained at riding in a saddle from an extremely early age. You gain the [Ride](/Feats.aspx?ID=835) feat. Additionally, you are not [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) while you [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33).
name: Shortshanks Hobgoblin

<title level="1" right="Heritage" pfs="Standard">[Steelskin Hobgoblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=96)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 33

When you are cut or burned, your body responds by forming tough but flexible callouses over the site of the injury. Over time, this makes the most vulnerable sections of your body harder and more resistant to damage. Many steelskin hobgoblins intentionally use blades and flame to scar themselves in intricate patterns, though this isn't universal. Your flat check to recover from [persistent physical (including bleed), energy, or poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is DC 13 (rather than DC 15), or DC 8 when provided particularly effective assistance.
name: Steelskin Hobgoblin

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 37

You live in a vertically-oriented home, and you're a consummate climber. You gain the [Combat Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=768) skill feat, even if you don't meet the prerequisites. You can use your clawed feet and tail to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), leaving your hands free. Additionally, if you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Climb, you critically succeed instead.
